description_long: This is an acoustic guitar with a natural wood finish, featuring a classic dreadnought body shape. The top is a light-colored wood, likely spruce, with a visible soundhole and a simple wooden bridge. The neck is long and slender, with a dark fingerboard and dot inlays marking the fret positions. The back and sides are also wood, displaying a subtle grain pattern, and the headstock is fitted with six tuning pegs arranged three on each side. The guitar lacks any visible electronics or pickups, emphasizing its acoustic nature. The overall craftsmanship appears clean and traditional, suitable for street performances or casual play in urban environments.
description: A natural-finish acoustic guitar with a classic dreadnought body, wooden bridge, and six tuning pegs. The design is simple and traditional, with visible wood grain and dot inlays.
description_view_0: Front view showing the light wood top, soundhole, bridge, fingerboard with dot inlays, and headstock with tuning pegs.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the guitar's depth, curved body, and the thickness of the wooden construction.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back's wood grain pattern, neck, and the back of the headstock with tuning pegs.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the same body depth and curvature as the other side.
attribute_headstock_design: Standard acoustic headstock with three tuning pegs on each side, no visible logo, and a simple rectangular shape.
